text: Former Ethereum Foundation Researchers Launch Ethlabs With Backing From Bitmine, SharpLink and Joe Lubin Ethlabs, a new nonprofit Ethereum research and development organization founded by former Ethereum Foundation researchers, has officially launched. The founding team includes Ansgar Dietrichs, Barnabé Monnot, Caspar Schwarz-Schilling, Josh Rudolf and Julian Ma, with backing from Bitmine, SharpLink, Joe Lubin, Anchorage, Octant and SNZ. Ethlabs will focus on Ethereum protocol research, including settlement efficiency, scalability, cross-chain interoperability, data availability and protocol economics. The organization said it will operate independently, with research priorities determined by its leadership and no influence from funders over its technical roadmap. — link

text: The Bank of England has published its final policy framework and draft rules for systemic stablecoins, easing several proposals from last year's consultation. The central bank scrapped plans to cap individual stablecoin holdings and instead proposed a total issuance limit per stablecoin, initially set at £40 billion. The BoE also slightly relaxed requirements on reserve backing assets and said it aims to finalize the rules by the end of 2026. Aave founder @StaniKulechov said the Bank of England's stablecoin framework remains too restrictive, arguing that requiring issuers to hold 30% of reserves in non-interest-bearing central bank accounts would hurt profitability and drive firms offshore. He warned the UK could struggle to compete with the U.S. under the proposed rules. — link

text: Highlight Clip Arthur Hayes: Selling Altcoins Because the AI Trade is About to Peak On June 13, 2026, Arthur Hayes stated in an interview with Cointelegraph that he had liquidated his altcoin positions, including HYPE, NEAR, and Worldcoin. Reflecting on his previous macro forecasts, he mentioned that current fiat liquidity did not flow into Bitcoin, but was instead entirely absorbed by the AI sector. Hayes believes the AI narrative is currently facing three problems: the vexing price of energy, the risk of US policy reversals, and the liquidity drain from the mega IPOs of Anthropic and OpenAI. He expressed that it is not as if AI goes down 50%, people have capital to invest in Bitcoin; rather, Bitcoin would get "thrown out with the bathwater." Given these risks are present, he decided to exit. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: South Korea's third-largest internet-only bank, Toss Bank, signed an MOU with the Solana Foundation in Seoul on June 19 to test global remittance and settlement infrastructure based on the Solana network and assess the potential use of stablecoins in overseas remittances. Toss Bank said it is the first one-to-one strategic partnership between a South Korean internet-only bank and the Solana Foundation, and plans to gradually review the feasibility of applying blockchain infrastructure to payments, digital assets and tokenized assets, starting with overseas remittances. — link

text: 'Brainless' AI discourse, the myth of capitalism and the Gen Z revolution risk Wall Street's AI economic projections are heavily overblown, estimating that AI will only deliver a modest 0.55% gain in total factor productivity over the next decade because current models fail at complex, real-world tasks Instead of focusing on unrealistic automation hype, he urges society to address how tech hyperscalers function as extractive institutions that concentrate corporate power, worsen inequality, and risk triggering political instability among displaced younger workers

text: Ukraine "e-points" system in its war with Russia Where drone units earn points for verified strikes on soldiers, tanks, depots, and other targets, redeemable for other equipments via the Brave1 online marketplace The incentive mechanism has driven success by focusing efforts on high-priority targets, creating unit leaderboards, and directing better gear to the most effective squadrons, contributing to deeper strikes into Russian territory after years of stalemate

text: Taiko said in a security notice that it has confirmed a compromise of its chain state verification mechanism, and that the security assumptions of all bridges deployed on Taiko can no longer be relied upon. Taiko strongly advised users to immediately withdraw funds from all relevant bridges and requested centralized exchanges to suspend TAIKO deposits until further official notice. Earlier, Blockaid said Taiko's ERC20 Vault on Ethereum had been attacked, with losses exceeding $1 million. Preliminary analysis showed the vulnerability stemmed from a flaw in the source-signal proof verification mechanism of Taiko's bridge. — link
author: admin#1534592031
